entry.title: Re: Subledger Source Document Type in Journal Entry Query
entry.content:
Hi @kedalenechong ,

Not sure if this is what you meant exactly, but:

OJDT.TransType: Specifies the type of transaction that generated the journal entry, such as an A/P invoice, or any other marketing document/payment. Link to the object type (check the list here: List of object types - SAP Business One - SAP Community).

OJDT.BaseRef: Contains the document number or other reference from the original business document (e.g., an invoice number). This, along with the TransType, helps identify the source document.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Limited Time Activation of virtual Super-User - SAP*
entry.content:
SAP Note 3303172

Time-limited client-specific activation of the virtual super-user SAP*, available as of kernel release 790:

  1. Logon to the operating system of an application server using <sid>adm user.
  2. Start the interactive tool dpmon with menu option u to activate the virtual client-specific super-user SAP* in a chosen client. Define a period of between 10 and 30 minutes as validity. You will obtain a one-time password after successful activation.
  3. Logon using user SAP* and the one-time password you have obtained on any currently running application server.

Point to be noted :

  • Any password based logon attempt for user SAP*, regardless if successful or not, invalidates the one-time password immediately.
  • You can (re-)activate the user using dpmon any time and will obtain a new one-time password. This is needed for example if you had a typo in your attempt to use the one-time password and could not logon.
  • Within dpmon you have further the possibility to see in which clients a virtual super-user does currently exist, which one still has a one-time password, their remaining existence and you are able to delete them prematurely.
  • An existing user SAP* (and an emergency super-user SAP* activated by the second option) is superimposed by an existing virtual super-user SAP* in a client.
  • A maximum of 20 virtual super-user SAP* can exist in parallel in different clients.
  • There is no need for an application server restart as no static profile parameter needs to be changed.
  • The user has no hardcoded known password but gets a new random one-time password after each activation.
  • The user is activated client-specific.
  • The user existence is limited in time, its validity is chosen during its activation within dpmon(the allowed period is between 10 and 30 minutes), and the user can also be deleted within dpmon already before its expiration. Bear in mind: a user deletion has no influence on an already established session. A session does continue to run.
  • Both activation and usage of the virtual super-user are logged in the security audit log event EUP (purpose 2).

 

Security Audit Log for virtual user SAP*

entry.title: Re: Ex Subj : My requirement is Excel file upload in Buil...
entry.content:
To upload an Excel file in SAP Build Apps and create a record, you can follow these steps:

Steps to Upload and Process Excel File

  1. File Upload Component:

    • Use a file upload component to allow users to select and upload the Excel file. This component can be added to your SAP Build Apps project.

  2. Backend Processing:

    • Once the file is uploaded, you need to process the file in the backend. You can use a service action to handle the file upload and parsing.
    • For parsing the Excel file, you can use libraries such as xlsx in Node.js or Java. This will help you read the content of the Excel file and convert it into a format that can be processed.

  3. Creating Records:

    • After parsing the Excel file, you can create records in your database. You can use batch inserts to efficiently handle large amounts of data.
    • Implement an optimized procedure for mass operations to avoid performance issues. Here is an example of how you can define and handle such an action in CAP (Cloud Application Programming):
service MyService {
  action massOperations(x: YourParameters) returns String;
}

srv.on('massOperations', async req => {
  // implement optimized procedure
  return "Success";
});

entry.title: Re: Subject : My requirement is Excel file upload in Buil...
entry.content:
Hello Mohit, thanks for reaching ou to us, Are you still encountering an issue ? if yes you can follow these steps: to upload an Excel file in SAP Build Apps and create a new record upon submission, 

Create a File Upload Component:

Add a file upload component to your page where users can select and upload their Excel files.
Handle File Upload:

Use the FileReader API to read the content of the uploaded Excel file. You can parse the file using a third-party library like xlsx from npm

Parse Excel Data:

Parse the Excel file data to extract the necessary information. This can be done using the xlsx package to convert the file content into a JSON format.
Create OData Batch Request:

Create an OData batch request to push the parsed data to the backend. This involves creating a service action in your CAP model to handle mass operations
service MyService {
action massOperations(x: YourParameters) returns String;
}

srv.on('massOperations', async req => {
// implement optimized procedure
return "Success";
});
Backend Processing:

Implement the backend logic to validate and insert/update the data into SAP HANA. You can refer to the example provided by Thomas Jung for mass operations
Submit Data:

Upon submission, ensure the data is sent to the backend service for processing. Handle any errors or validation issues appropriately.

entry.title: Re: Getting error while installing Desktop Agent 3
entry.content:
Hello Jagadhatri, Are you still encountering an issue? please follow below steps to Install the desktop Agent 

    • For all users:
      • Open the downloaded SAP Desktop Agent file and follow the setup wizard instructions.
      • Ensure the agent is installed in the folder %programfiles%\SAP\SAPDesktopAgent 
    • For the current user:
      • Open the downloaded SAP Desktop Agent file and follow the setup wizard instructions.
      • Open the downloaded SAP Desktop Agent Service Setup file and follow the setup wizard instructions.
      • Ensure the files are installed in the folder %localappdata%\Programs\SAPDesktopAgent 

  2. Uninstall and Reinstall:

    • If switching from all users mode to per current user mode, uninstall the previous installation first before reinstalling
